commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ef98ed644ab9ca1c079dcb5eb176a6a5f9b06f7a)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a normalized QA evidence summary schema, embeds optional evidence blocks in QA suite and Telegram live summary artifacts, and adds focused schema/runtime tests.

PR surface: Source +599, Tests +401. Total +1000 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: set OPENCLAW_QA_PROFILE or OPENCLAW_E2E_PROFILE to an unsupported value and the new parser throws before the QA suite or Telegram artifact writers emit their summaries. I did not run the lane because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers should choose the evidence profile compatibility contract and stacked taxonomy landing order before a repair worker changes the behavior.

target.title: Add QA evidence summary normalization
target.body:
## Summary

- Align the QA evidence summary schema with RFC 0007's two-profile model: `profile` is only `smoke-ci` or `release`, with explicit `model_live`, `channel_live`, provider fixture/auth, channel driver, and runner fields.
- Keep taxonomy provenance out of per-run evidence summaries; evidence entries carry scorecard surface/category IDs and join through the checked-in mapping/taxonomy layer.
- Embed the foundational `evidence` block in QA suite and Telegram live summaries without adding the scorecard gate; summary files continue to live under the command `--output-dir` or the default `.artifacts/qa-e2e/<lane>-<timestamp>` path.
- Normalize legacy profile env aliases into the current schema output so retained operator env values do not emit old profile names.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/evidence-summary.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.summary-json.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite-summary.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- live PR diff check for personal-name owner references

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: QA Lab outputs now include an RFC 0007-aligned evidence summary shape, and Telegram live QA writes the same top-level evidence schema as suite summaries.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex worktree on macOS, with focused unit/schema tests.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused evidence summary, suite summary JSON, suite summary rendering, and Telegram live runtime tests through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`, plus whitespace and live PR diff checks.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 4 files / 65 tests; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed; the live PR diff contains no personal-name owner references.

Observed result after fix: QA suite evidence defaults deterministic mock-provider proof to `profile: "smoke-ci"`, `model_live: false`, `channel_live: false`, and `provider_fixture: "mock-openai"`. Telegram live evidence defaults to `profile: "release"`, `model_live: true`, `provider_auth: "live-frontier"`, `channel_live: true`, and `channel_driver: "native"`. Evidence summaries keep `channel_driver` as opaque channel-driver metadata while `runner` remains the execution substrate.

What was not tested: No scorecard gate was added or run, no channel-driver SDK lane was implemented, and no live Telegram provider lane was executed for this scoped schema/output-shape change.
target.author: romneyda
